response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and systems software developer with 14+ years of experience across web, mobile, and embedded domains, but his background has virtually no overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position requires deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator optimization, hardware-software co-design, and deep neural network workloads — none of which appear in his resume. His closest relevant work (low-latency DLang services handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Sociomantic and robotic arm development at Altom) is too peripheral to compensate for the complete absence of hardware architecture, GPU workload analysis, or ML/DL performance engineering experience.
strengths: 14+ years of professional software engineering with demonstrated exposure to high-throughput, low-latency systems (millions of HTTP requests/second at Sociomantic using async I/O and fiber-based concurrency)
Hardware-adjacent work including robotic arm software development and a bachelor thesis on wave field synthesis using C++ and multi-channel audio hardware
Active open-source contributor and community educator, indicating strong communication skills relevant to the cross-functional partner feedback responsibilities of a principal-level architect
concerns: No SoC or IP modeling experience of any kind — the primary technical requirement of the role is entirely absent from his background
No AI/ML or deep-learning workload expertise; current AI-adjacent work is limited to MCP tooling and agentic workflow infrastructure, not accelerator or DNN performance optimization
Skill set is dominated by web and application technologies (TypeScript, Node.js, MongoDB, React) with no evidence of GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD programming, or hardware-software co-design

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture experience
No deep learning or AI accelerator background
Long career with performance-sensitive software engineering
